title: Medica littoralis

parts:
Medica littoralis is also known as Lomatium. 
Medica littoralis, or Lomatium, is used in herbal medicine and its medicinal parts include its roots and leaves. 
The roots of Lomatium are rich in sesquiterpenes and alkaloids, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. 
The leaves of Lomatium are also used to make tea, which is used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and colds. 
However, it is essential to note that Lomatium can be toxic in large doses and should be used under the guidance of a medical professional.

intro: Medica littoralis, also known as Prickly Samphire or Sea Asparagus, is a type of succulent halophyte plant that thrives in coastal environments. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a popular remedy for various cardiovascular and respiratory issues. Medica littoralis contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, this plant is commonly prepared as a decoction, tincture, or infused in tea form to take advantage of its therapeutic benefits.
